    6. Read This Before You Do THE DRILL DOWN METHOD
</h2>

  <!-- Attachment Blocks -->
<!-- DEBUG: attachments.count=2 -->

  <!-- DEBUG: attachment.id=64918514, kind=text -->

<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><h1><strong style="background-color: transparent;">PART I. ARE YOU READY FOR ALL THIS "MESSAGE" BUSINESS?</strong></h1><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Ok, let me ask you two questions.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">The first one is:</span></p><h2><span style="background-color: transparent;">Why do you make art?</span></h2><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">If you answered:</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">1. "I make art for fun."</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">OR</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">2. "I make art for arts sake."</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent; color: var(--squid-ink);">Then you might still be in self-discovery mode right now. Why overload that part of the journey by trying to attach a message to it? </span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Eventually, a burning sense of purpose does start to take over your work. A time when making "pretty things", or "making hot beats" is no longer enough... </span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Until that passion rises, don't force it.</span></p><p><em style="background-color: transparent;">But if you DO feel that fire in your belly, then you're in the right place.</em></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Now tell me...</span></p><h2><span style="background-color: transparent;">Who do you make your art for?</span></h2><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">If you answered</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">1. "Everybody."</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">OR</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">2. </span>"I make art for myself, and if someone else likes it then great."</p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Again, maybe you should hold off on "finding your message"?</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">A message only comes in handy when you're ready to communicating an artistic vision to "your people."</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">If you believe that you're making your art for "everybody"... Then you definitely DON'T want your art to have a message.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Why?</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">When you have a message you run the daily risk of offending one of those "everybody's" in our hypersensitive culture.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">And if it's a really POWERFUL message... Damn, you might even "get cancelled".</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Mainstream audiences -- the "everybody's" that many people want to appeal to -- they don't like to be challenged by a "unique artistic vision". The masses prefer the cozy comfort zone of what they already know. They prefer the work of craftspeople and technicians. They are late-adopters to artists.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Hold off on this whole "message business" until you're ready to ruffle some feathers, ok? </span></p><p>And if you make art "for yourself"... Then you definitely don't need to worry about communicating anything to anyone. You're in "Me, myself and I" mode. You don't need a message. All good.</p><h2>But for everybody else... </h2><p>If you're still reading then you're one of the rare ones who have come to peace with this: </p><p><strong>ART = CREATIVE SELF EXPRESSION IN SERVICE OF UPLIFTING A PARTICULAR COMMUNITY</strong></p><p><strong>That's what Lady Gaga does. That's what Frida Khalo does. That's what Rupi Kaur does. That's what ALL legends do.</strong></p><p>And this whole "finding your message" journey is about:</p><ol>
<li>Identifying your community</li>
<li>and defining the artistic vision you want to communicate to them.</li>
</ol><p><em>Ok. Glad we got that out of the way.</em></p><h1><strong style="background-color: transparent;">PART II. DON'T EXPECT A TROPHY AT THE END</strong></h1><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">If you think you're gonna do all the exercises in 'The Book Of Purpose' and that at the end of it all, a little ticker tape will spit out your message... And everyone will cheer. Bad news. It's way more lonely than that. </span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Purpose is like love: It's generated by passion.</span></p><h2><span style="background-color: transparent;">Nothing happens to you "at the end". Everything that's going to happen to you will happen throughout the process.</span></h2><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">More bad news: Nothing here will generate that passion for you.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">More good news: The Drill Down Method will bring out everything that's already there, but been hiding.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Searching for your message is a process. A journey. Just like making art is.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">And even though I split this experience into 7 days... Understand that it may take longer or shorter for you.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">They key is to only attempt this when you can give it your full attention. Don't rush through. Never gloss over anything. I mean, come on... There's a reason it's called The Drill Down Method. You're supposed to go deep. </span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">You're trying to hit a nerve.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">If you get stuck somewhere and you find you can't answer a fundamental question. Don't move forward. Stay in the mud.</span></p><blockquote>
<span style="background-color: transparent;"> </span><em style="background-color: transparent;">"It's time for you to answer the questions you've been avoiding. It's time you find the big juicy answers that fire you the fuck up. Find some solitude-- Bring a bag of Doritos and some tea. Lock yourself in that room until you uncover something new. The first answers will be shallow. Allow them to be. Allow for the possibility that you may not like ANY of the answers right away. Or that they may make you rethink your whole direction. Yes, find a place of solitude-- Bring some hummus, crackers and a glass of white wine. And if the answer doesn't come right away... Stare outside your window. Don't stop early because you're bored, like you've done so many times in the past. Let the question linger this time. In mid-air. It'll be uncomfortable at first. You've trained your mind to sweep all uncomfortable feelings under the rug. But, I'm telling you... Have the courage to leave the questions out in the open for a little longer... Go to sleep with the unanswered questions. Wake up with them. Stay unsettled for a few days. Have confidence in the process. Keep it at the forefront of your mind, and let it submerge into your unconscious. That's how you solve it... You'll be astonished to see what appears. How do I know that something will happen? Because The mind hates a vacuum. And if you don't settle for the first thing that jumps out at you... the mind will use every resource to find the answers you're looking for. I promise you. -- Father Bronques</em>
</blockquote><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Everything else in your life is instant gratification. That's the culture these days. But this process requires you to end the habit of staying on the surface of things.</span></p><p><em style="background-color: transparent;">If you want the truth, that is.</em></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Be ok with sitting with an unanswered question for many nights if that's what it takes.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">"Keep the window open."</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Hey.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">You're trying to reveal your artistic vision. </span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">We're trying to get you to put it into words the reason your art exists-- So your audience can be riveted by it.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Isn't that end result worth the highest level of introspection?</span></p><p><br></p><h1><strong style="background-color: transparent;">PART III. YOU GET WHAT YOU GIVE</strong></h1><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">The Drill Down Method will only be as powerful as your partner is willing to go down the rabbit hole with you. </span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">The Drill Down Method will only be as powerful as HOW MUCH YOU'RE WILLING TO "LET GO" AND REVEAL.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">The Drill Down Method is like therapy.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">If you hold back... Nothing will come out of it.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">If your partner is afraid to call you on your bullshit... Same. Nothing will come out.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">It's a lot like art.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Or like life.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">You get what you give.</span></p><p><br></p><h1><strong style="background-color: transparent;">PART IV. FOCUS ON THE JOURNEY.</strong></h1><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">I heard from two students this year who thought their Drill Down was a failure.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">They were so focused on "I didn't find my message"-- that they totally missed what they HAD found.</span></p><p><em style="background-color: transparent;">Take Isabella, for example.</em></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">She didn't "find her message". But she discovered her Polaris Star.</span></p><p><em style="background-color: transparent;">In her words:</em></p><p><strong style="background-color: transparent;"><em>Dear Father Bronques, </em></strong></p><p><strong style="background-color: transparent;"><em>Before The Drill Down Method, I had pretty much decided on being a painter. But around DAY 2 of Drill Down, I noticed that MUSIC kept popping up over and over again. I quickly realized that if I didn't follow my music passion, I would seriously regret it. So I made a choice to switch paths even though it feels like I'm starting a whole new journey.</em></strong></p><p><strong style="background-color: transparent;"><em>Those five days showed me things I was unaware of. It made me realize that I wasn't struggling with WHAT I wanted to say after all... I had to figure out HOW I wanted to say it. I know that I don't have THEE message right yet, but now that I'm following my true passion, I feel like everything I'm doing is leading to it anyways. I see it coming out in what I sing and write about already. </em></strong></p><p><strong style="background-color: transparent;"><em>I appreciate all that you do. Thank you for taking the time to read. I'm open to any suggestions if you think there's something I'm not seeing. Have a beautiful rest of your day! </em></strong></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Not at all, Isabelle... You're seeing everything you need to see.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">When you get a major epiphany, don't be afraid to stop and go explore it before you continue.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Don't feel guilty about that. </span></p><h2><span style="background-color: transparent;">Your journey is not supposed to be linear.</span></h2><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Come back here later when needing to find your message hits you in the face again (if your current epiphany doesn't lead you there all by itself).</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">The other example I wanted to share with you is Mayra's experience.</span></p><p><em style="background-color: transparent;">Here is our back and forth:</em></p><p><strong style="background-color: transparent;"><em>Dear Father Bronques,</em></strong></p><p><strong style="background-color: transparent;"><em>I tried to do the Drill Down Method again (2nd time). I still don "t quite feel like I've arrived at my message. Lately, I've been beating up on myself and getting frustrated. </em></strong></p><p><strong style="background-color: transparent;"><em>FATHER BRONQUES: Mayra, let's investigate... How far did you get in The Drill Down? </em></strong></p><p><strong style="background-color: transparent;"><em>MAYRA: First time we got to Day 3, the second time to Day 5</em></strong></p><p><strong style="background-color: transparent;"><em>FATHER BRONQUES: You can't get to Day 5 without revealing SOMETHING. What did you get so far? </em></strong></p><p><strong style="background-color: transparent;"><em>MAYRA: "Everyone is (message excluded for privacy). The world requires us to (omitted). Rejecting the boxes and (omitted). I want to make music that is (omitted). </em></strong></p><p><strong style="background-color: transparent;"><em>FATHER BRONQUES: Mayra, WTF? "Everyone is (omitted)" is already a sick little contrarian slogan. Everything else you wrote is already a great foundation to create a brand identity around. It's so YOU. I mean... Can it be fine-tuned? Of course. Do you still need to figure out your audience and how to tie it all into the music you're making? Yeah, no doubt. But THIS is more than enough to build an online presence that's cohesive and aligned with purpose. </em></strong></p><p><strong style="background-color: transparent;"><em>MAYRA: Damn, you're right. I had a look through my insta feed and you really can't tell that I am about any of these things. And they are all truly me. You're the first person ever to encourage me to bet on myself. It's scary, but I know it has to be done now. I honestly thought these messages weren't "good enough". As I'm saying it out loud, though, I realize how silly that sounds. I'm starting to understand that this is really just a process:</em></strong></p><ul>
<li><strong style="background-color: transparent;"><em>Figuring out what works.</em></strong></li>
<li><strong style="background-color: transparent;"><em>Adapting.</em></strong></li>
<li><strong style="background-color: transparent;"><em>Trying something new.</em></strong></li>
<li><strong style="background-color: transparent;"><em>Getting feedback and chipping away.</em></strong></li>
</ul><p><strong style="background-color: transparent;"><em>*a few days later, I heard from Mayra again...*</em></strong></p><p><strong style="background-color: transparent;"><em>MAYRA: I started recording vocals for the first time yesterday. No words yet, just sounds, harmonies, etc. It's a whole different feeling now. I also started sending out emails to my email list of 56 people too :) Everything already feels more "right". Thank you for your words.</em></strong></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Don't focus on finding a "perfect message".</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Focus instead on the epiphanies that you get everyday.</span></p><p><em style="background-color: transparent;">Those epiphanies will tell you where you need to go next.</em></p><p><br></p><h1><strong style="background-color: transparent;">PART V. IF YOU DON'T ACT ON YOUR MESSAGE, YOU'VE WASTED YOUR TIME</strong></h1><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">I don't care what beautiful "message sentence," you write down at the end of the 7 days... If your whole demeanor doesn't reflect a newfound clarity and purpose... If you're not propelled into action, then you've wasted your time. </span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Don't start Drill Down if you don't think you have the energy to "move different" afterwards.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Dear student,</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">When you REALLY find your message...</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Food tastes different.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">You start to question what type of people you've been hanging out with.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">You don't want to waste time anymore. Every second is precious.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">You have a burning purpose. You dream about it.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">When you look at your Instagram feed, you immediately see everything you need to do to make it more YOU.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">You make your website a billboard for your artistic vision.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Every newsletter you send to your list reeks of someone burning with forward momentum.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Even your personal emails are fired up. You might even find yourself adding a new "signature" at the bottom to express "the new you".</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">You're on a mission.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">You have more to say than you can possibly share, so you start needing to stagger your online output.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">You struggle with NOT making every conversation at dinner about your passion.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">But the best part is this:</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">When people ask you about your art-- the first words out of your mouth is about WHY you make your art not about how good it is. And because of this, even people outside of your community get excited-- before they even have a chance to experience it.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">So if you still think you still want to "go there"... </span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Allow 'The Drill Down Method' to give you a few epiphanies or reinforce the convictions you were on the fence about.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">BUT PLEASE--</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">While you're doing it... Don't go through the motions.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Don't gloss over questions. Don't skip hard to answer ones. </span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">And don't go into it thinking that something is going to "happen to you".</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">No one's bringing you a fortune cookie at the end with your message printed out for you to follow.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">If you DO have an artistic vision... This process will reveal it. </span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">And it will give you the fortitude to pursue it.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">But don't wait to apply it.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Don't dare let yourself go back to "business as usual" after this intense process reveals your truth!</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Act on it right away.</span></p><p><span style="background-color: transparent;">Before the outside forces convince you that mediocrity and fitting in is the best path again.</span></p><p><em style="background-color: transparent;">Only ACTION can sustain even the strongest epiphany.</em></p><p></p>
</div>
